methods.description: Process for pre-composting plant residues and solarized sewage sludge to prepare for vermicomposting.

steps.description: Pre-composting was performed from September to November 2014, in a total of seven weeks, using, in volume, three parts of plant residues from the campus garden pruning for one part of solarized sewage sludge obtained in the STP of Montes Claros, MG.
steps.searchable_summary: mix plant residues with solarized sewage sludge for pre-composting

steps.description: The material was turned once a week along the period of pre-composting.

methods.description: Vermicomposting using a randomized complete block design with different treatments of DE residue and pre-compost mixtures.

steps.description: Vermicomposting was performed from November 2014 to April 2015, in a total of four months, using a randomized complete block statistical design with five treatments and five replicates.
steps.searchable_summary: set up vermicomposting experiment with block design

steps.description: The treatments consisted in the mixture of DE residue with pre-compost (PC) produced at the following proportions (%, v/v): T1 -0:100; T2 -7.53:92.47; T3 -15.06:84.94; T4 -22.59:77.41; T5 -30.12:69.88, respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: mix DE residue with pre-compost in specified proportions

steps.description: Each experimental unit received 1 kg of redworms (Eisenia foetida).
steps.searchable_summary: add redworms to each experimental unit

steps.description: The containers were covered with shade cloth to avoid excessive evaporation or drying of the substrate, protect the earthworms from predators and prevent their escape.
Substrate moisture was daily measured by hand pressing the samples and observing if the water drained or if the sample was dry.
steps.searchable_summary: measure substrate moisture daily by hand pressing

steps.description: Water was replenished to maintain the moisture close to the substrate water holding capacity, by returning the drained excess of water to the container.
steps.searchable_summary: replenish water to maintain substrate moisture

steps.description: The mixture was monthly turned by hand, to avoid damages to the earthworms and compaction, facilitating aeration and drainage.

methods.description: Chemical analysis of the substrate to determine various nutrient and metal contents.

steps.description: After four months, samples of the substrate produced in each experimental unit were taken to the Laboratories of Analyses of Residues for Agricultural Use (LARAA) and of Agrochemistry Research (LPA) of the ICA/UFMG, to determine the contents of N and Al 3+ (Tedesco et al., 1995), pH, cation exchange capacity (CEC), contents of K, P, S, Na, organic carbon (Alcarde, 2009), As, Pb, Cr, Co, Ni, Fe, Ca, Mg, Mo, Cu, Zn, Ba, Cd, Mn, Al and Se (EPA, 1994).
steps.searchable_summary: analyze substrate samples for nutrient and metal contents

methods.description: Statistical procedures used to analyze the collected data and test significance.

steps.description: The obtained results were subjected to analysis of variance and regression equations were fitted, testing the significance of the coefficients up to 0.10 probability level by t-test using the R software version 3.2.0.
steps.searchable_summary: perform ANOVA and regression analysis using R
